channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Copper price eases as traders weigh Fed's next move
item.description: Copper is consolidating after a two-month surge driven by speculative buying that took it to a record high at the end of January. Minutes from the Fed's January meeting indicated policymakers were wary of cutting rates, which weighed on the demand outlook for metals. Renewed US-Iran risks also pressured demand for risky assets including metals.

item.title: [SMM News Flash] On March 17, SMM reported that the ex-factory...
item.description: Nickel prices and stainless steel futures remained elevated this week, with bullish sentiment driving continuous increases in high-grade NPI (nickel pig iron) prices. The market continues to show strength amid ongoing supply and demand dynamics.

item.title: Aluminum prices in March 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices on LME have surged over 22% year-to-date in 2026, outpacing gold's 15% rise, amid Middle East geopolitical tensions. The Strait of Hormuz, vital for 20% of global aluminum production outside China, is a key factor driving the shock increase.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Signals Industrial Resurgence: Q1 2026 Guidance Nearly Doubles Previous Year's Performance
item.description: Steel Dynamics issued strong Q1 2026 guidance, signaling robust demand outstripping domestic production capacity amid infrastructure and data center booms. Mini-mill operators like Steel Dynamics and Nucor are poised to benefit from onshoring trends and federal funding. Imported steel-reliant manufacturers may face higher costs.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Provides First Quarter 2026 Earnings Guidance
item.description: Steel Dynamics forecasts Q1 2026 earnings of $2.73 to $2.77 per diluted share, driven by higher steel shipments, metal margin expansion, and strong demand in non-residential construction, energy, automotive, and industrial sectors. Steel fabrication earnings remain steady with a 35% higher order backlog, supported by infrastructure and onshoring. The company advances commissioning of its new aluminum flat rolled products mill in Mississippi.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Provides First Quarter 2026 Earnings Guidance
item.description: Steel Dynamics expects higher Q1 2026 profitability from steel operations due to increased shipments and expanded margins, with strong demand from key sectors. Metals recycling earnings rise on higher selling values despite lower scrap shipments from weather impacts. Fabrication volumes grow with a significantly larger order backlog into Q3 2026 amid infrastructure support.

item.title: Copper futures fell as Chinese demand softened
item.description: Copper futures declined on March 17, 2026, amid weakening physical demand in China, with fabricator buying slumping and the Yangshan import premium at its lowest since mid-2024. This marked the fourth decline in five trading days, reversing a brief rally as traders focused on softened consumption.

item.title: [SMM Daily Review of Nickel Sulphate] March 17, Trading Sentiment Was Relatively Weak, and Nickel Salt Prices Remained Stable
item.description: Trading sentiment for nickel sulphate was relatively weak on March 17. Nickel salt prices held steady despite the subdued market mood. This stability occurred amid broader nickel market fluctuations.

item.title: U.S. Steel Production Gains Despite Weekly Dip: AISI
item.description: U.S. domestic raw steel production totaled 1.774 million net tons for the week ending March 14, 2026, with a capacity utilization rate of 76.7%, down slightly from the prior week but up 4.2% year-over-year. Year-to-date production through March 14 reached 18.613 million net tons at 77.2% utilization, a 4.9% increase from 2025. Regional output was led by the Southern district at 795,000 net tons.

item.title: Tata Steel and USTB Forge Path Toward Low-Carbon Steelmaking
item.description: Tata Steel has signed an MoU with the University of Science and Technology Beijing (USTB) to collaborate on low-carbon steelmaking technologies. The partnership focuses on scrap-based steelmaking, steel waste valorization, end-product performance, and carbon capture, leveraging USTB's facilities to pilot scalable solutions. This initiative supports the global transition to sustainable steel production.[2]

item.title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers
item.description: As of March 16, nickel was trading at $17,428/tonne, up 0.62% on the day, supported by Indonesia's supply restraint and tighter ore availability. Nickel has risen 3.55% over the past month and 6.20% year-over-year, showing stronger medium-term performance despite recent volatility.

item.title: U.S. Steel Production Up 4.2% Year-on-Year for Week Ending March 14, 2026
item.description: U.S. domestic raw steel production reached 1.774 million net tons for the week ending March 14, 2026, with a capacity utilization rate of 76.7%. This represents a 4.2% increase compared to the same week in 2025, though it reflects a marginal 0.9% decline from the prior week. Year-to-date production through March 14, 2026, totaled 18.613 million net tons, marking a 4.9% rise from the same period in 2025.

item.title: U.S. Weekly Steel Output Jumps 4.2% Year-on-Year: AISI
item.description: U.S. mills produced 1.774 million net tons for the week ending March 14, 2026, up 4.2% year-over-year but down 0.9% week-over-week from March 7, 2026. The data indicates the industry has moved beyond last year's trough, with year-to-date production reaching 18.613 million net tons at 77.2% average utilization, a 4.9% gain compared to the same period in 2025.

item.title: Small scale demolitions and piling work start at EAF sites
item.description: Tata Steel UK continues its decarbonization transformation at the Port Talbot site, with ongoing demolition of redundant assets and groundworks preparation for new construction projects. This work is part of the company's broader effort to secure the long-term future of steel production in South Wales.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 18.03.2026
item.description: SteelRadar provides updates on global iron and steel prices, including steady Shanghai Futures rebar and HRC prices on March 17, 2026. Key news highlights Türkiye as a transit hub for Chinese steel to Europe, Czech steel industry crisis, and Nucor raising HRC prices by $5/st. CBAM deadline of March 31 poses risks for non-compliant companies.

item.title: Leading Companies Fueling Growth and Innovation in the Copper Foil Market
item.description: The copper foil market is forecasted to reach $22.54 billion by 2030 with a 10.0% CAGR, driven by advancements in high-performance copper foils for energy storage and electronics. Industry innovations include AI-powered quality control, IoT-connected manufacturing lines, and sustainable copper foil manufacturing practices, with strategic partnerships like Varta and SK Nexilis advancing EV battery technologies.

item.title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Rises Toward US$17,420/Ton Level (Tuesday, March 17, 2026)
item.description: The 3-month forward nickel price rose 0.49% to US$17,420 per ton on March 17, 2026, marking the highest weekly jump and a 2.99% year-to-date gain. Weekly average prices stood at US$17,480 per ton with 0.2% growth. Annually, prices trend downward over five years, but monthly monitoring shows rises, peaking at US$18,030 in January 2026.

item.title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
item.description: Prysmian shares rose as much as 4% on expectations that the world's largest cable maker's large U.S. business could benefit from U.S. tariffs on copper, as it sources the metal locally and can pass on higher prices. Copper is key for power grids, construction, and high-voltage cables, with Prysmian as the largest global buyer and U.S. leader. Analysts at Citi highlight its vertically integrated U.S. setup using local copper from Arizona and Texas as a competitive edge.

item.title: Nickel Prices Rebounded on March 18
item.description: Nickel prices rebounded on March 18, 2026, with market participants noting that the US Fed may be more inclined to remain silent this week. This rebound follows earlier price retreats and reflects ongoing sensitivity to macroeconomic factors and monetary policy expectations.

item.title: Steel Consuming Market Outlook March 2026
item.description: Construction planning is cooling in early 2026 but remains at historically high levels. Residential housing construction is rebounding with January 2026 housing starts up 7.2% from December, driven by steel-intensive multi-family projects, though HVAC equipment shipments remain under pressure.

item.title: How the Iran war is squeezing metals markets and key industries
item.description: The Iran war disrupts Middle East aluminum supply, which provides 21% of unwrought and 13% of wrought imports, pushing prices to 4-year highs. Producers like Aluminium Bahrain and others face raw material shortages, with smelter restarts requiring 12 months. Chinese control over global aluminum prices may limit sustained gains.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Rise Amid Iran War
item.description: Aluminum Bahrain, the world's largest smelter, has cut production by 19% due to the ongoing Middle East war. CRU Group notes rising prices from these disruptions. The conflict continues to impact global aluminum supply chains.

item.title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Futures Price Falls to US$17,165/Ton (Wednesday, March 18, 2026)
item.description: The 3-month nickel commodity futures price fell to US$17,165 per ton on March 18, 2026, down from US$17,420 the previous day. Over the last week, prices declined by 1.77% with an average daily trading price of US$17,440 per ton, though prices remain up 1.48% compared to the start of 2026.

item.title: Global race as market for high-grade iron ore grows
item.description: Global demand for high-grade iron ores is rising as the steel sector pursues decarbonization, leading to a looming supply deficit. This trend intensifies competition in the market for premium iron ore grades essential for lower-emission steel production.

item.title: Global high-grade iron ore market is set to grow
item.description: The global high-grade iron ore market is projected to expand, potentially impacting low-emissions steel production. DR-grade iron ore could become a constraint as steelmakers shift toward greener processes requiring higher-quality inputs.

item.title: US flat steel pricing continues climb, lower March scrap could be balanced by low steel imports
item.description: Weekly US flat steel prices continued their recent ascent, with hot-rolled coils finishing the week up another $10/nt. Scrap prices, which rose $30/gt, could finish near or less than $395-405/gt. The article notes that lower March scrap levels could be balanced by low steel imports.

item.title: [SMM Nickel Midday Review] On March 19, nickel prices plunged sharply, and the US Fed kept interest rates unchanged at 3.5%-3.75%
item.description: Nickel prices plunged sharply on March 19 amid the US Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged at 3.5%-3.75%. This midday review highlights the significant drop in nickel prices. Market confidence was impacted, as noted in related NPI reviews.

item.title: [NPI Daily Review] Sharp Drop in Nickel Prices Weighed on Market Confidence, High-Grade NPI Price Center Pulled Back
item.description: A sharp drop in nickel prices on March 19 weighed heavily on market confidence, leading to a pullback in high-grade NPI prices. The review details the impact on nickel pig iron markets. This reflects broader pressures in the nickel sector.

item.title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed — March 19, 2026
item.description: Copper scrap prices fell $0.19 per pound across major grades on the ScrapMonster Price Index, with COMEX copper settling at $548 per pound (down 4.9%) and LME 3-month at $12,395.50 per ton (down 2.97%). The decline aligns with a stronger US dollar, demand concerns from rising energy costs, and geopolitical disruptions. Traders watch key support levels amid potential further downside.

item.title: Copper Drops $0.19 | March 19, 2026
item.description: Copper scrap prices dropped $0.19 per pound across major grades, marking the biggest market move that day. This reflects broader declines in copper futures due to macroeconomic pressures.

item.title: Copper wipes out 2026 gains as iran war punishes metals markets
item.description: Copper prices slumped below $12,000 per tonne in London, erasing year-to-date gains as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East negatively impacted metals markets.

item.title: How Trump's steel and aluminum tariffs impact prices one year later
item.description: One year after Trump's steel and aluminum tariffs, domestic steel shipments are up nearly 5%, but consumer prices for steel mill products have risen over 20% and aluminum nearly 40%. The tariffs remain intact despite some being struck down, leading to mixed industry results and higher costs.

item.title: China Cu prices fall on higher stocks, stronger dollar
item.description: Copper prices in China retreated in March due to rising inventories and a firmer US dollar, with the SHFE April contract falling to a three-month low of 95,400 yuan/t on March 18. Combined LME and SHFE inventories rose 56% to 745,283t by March 13, reflecting higher refined output and slower demand recovery post-Lunar New Year. LME three-month copper also declined over the period.

item.title: Copper Futures Decline Across Contracts in March 2026 Session
item.description: Copper futures traded lower, with the most active May 2026 contract settling at $5.3845 per pound, down $0.2095, amid lower trading volume and open interest. Prices for contracts into 2027 and 2028 also declined. The session reflects ongoing market pressures in March 2026.

item.title: Inside a $1.1B deal to reshore critical minerals refining
item.description: Nth Cycle's electrochemical refining technology for nickel, cobalt, and other minerals from battery scrap enables a $1.1B deal with Trafigura to expand US capacity fourfold. The compact system reduces capital costs and waste compared to traditional refineries, addressing reliance on Indonesia and China. It processes black mass from recycled batteries, keeping valuable resources domestic.

item.title: From production to installation: Tenaris brings integrated solutions to MCE 2026
item.description: Tenaris is showcasing its portfolio of hot-rolled seamless pipes for civil and industrial applications at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy (March 24-27). The company highlights its integrated European supply chain, with the Piombino facility playing a central role in providing galvanized, epoxy-coated, and polyethylene-coated pipes with recent Environmental Product Declaration certifications.

item.title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed
item.description: Copper led scrap metal market declines, falling $0.19 per pound across major grades. LME 3-month copper closed at $12,395.50 per ton, down 2.97%, pressured by a stronger US dollar and demand concerns tied to rising energy costs and geopolitical disruptions.

item.title: Acciaierie d'Italia to start preferential negotiations with Azerbaijani consortium on acquisition of former Ilva plants
item.description: Extraordinary commissioners of Acciaierie d’Italia and ILVA in extraordinary administration have requested authorization from Italy’s Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy to begin preferential negotiations with an Azerbaijani consortium (Baku Steel Company CJSC and Azerbaijan Investment Company OJSC) for the sale of the former Ilva steel plants.[8] Ten proposals had been received by the January 10 deadline, and the chosen consortium was selected based on criteria including financial stability, industrial sustainability, and expected benefits for employment and local communities.[8]

item.title: European Steel Industry News - Weekly Podcast
item.description: EU steel demand is stabilizing after three years of contraction, though record import penetration and political opposition to carbon costs complicate the recovery. The UK has unveiled a landmark Steel Strategy with aggressive trade measures, while rising prices across rebar and stainless flats signal tightening supply, with mill lead times stretching into late May.

item.title: Copper Supply Crunch Intensifies: Impact on Global Renewable Energy Sector
item.description: China's top copper smelters agreed to cut production by over 10% for 2026 due to collapsed Treatment and Refining Charges, indicating insufficient raw ore availability. Copper prices on the London Metal Exchange have reached a volatile range of $12,000 to $14,500 per metric ton as the global economy faces a structural supply deficit affecting the renewable energy transition.

item.title: Aluminum Prices on a Sustained Rise – Industry Trends and Best Practices
item.description: China's electrolytic aluminum capacity is capped at 45 million tons, with 44.43 million tons operational by end-2025 and no net additions expected in 2026. Overseas growth is limited by high electricity costs in Europe/North America and infrastructure issues elsewhere. Middle East tensions, accounting for 9% of global output, have caused production curtailments and shipping delays via the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Copper Drops to 3-Month Low
item.description: Copper fell to 5.34 USD/Lbs on March 20, 2026, down 1.67% from the previous day, marking a three-month low amid a strong dollar and concerns over high energy prices denting manufacturing demand. Over the past month, prices have declined 7.58%. Copper futures tracked declines in key industrial metals.

item.title: Copper Heads for Third Weekly Drop
item.description: Copper futures rebounded to around $5.5 per pound on Friday after hitting multi-month lows, supported by reassurances from the US and Israel on the Middle East conflict. US President Donald Trump stated no ground troops against Iran, while Israeli PM Netanyahu confirmed no further strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Prices are heading for a third weekly drop.

item.title: Al price forecasts hit $4,000/t on Middle East conflict
item.description: LME three-month aluminium prices reached $3,372/t due to Middle East conflict disrupting supplies via the Strait of Hormuz. Forecasts now predict prices above $4,000/t amid production stoppages by Qatalum, Alba, and others. Regional premiums in Europe and the US are surging on tight supply.

item.title: US flat steel prices mostly higher amid solid demand, Mideast war could stall
item.description: US flat steel prices were mostly higher due to solid demand, though Mideast war risks could stall momentum. This reflects ongoing weekly price climbs in the US flat steel market.

item.title: Weekly Market Recap (March 21) – Copper Prices Surge Past $11,000
item.description: Copper futures on COMEX surged past $11,000 per ton to $11,270 amid US tariff threats under President Trump, while LME prices crossed $10,000 with a widening premium. This has redirected over 100,000 tons of copper to the US, creating regional shortages and bullish sentiment. Persistent raw material shortages and potential smelter cuts are exacerbating supply concerns.

item.title: Nickel fell to 16885 USD/T on March 20, 2026
item.description: Nickel prices fell to 16,885 USD/T on March 20, 2026, down 1.05% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have declined 2.31%, though they remain 4.16% higher year-over-year. Nickel futures hit a 4-week low of 16,817 USD/T on March 19 amid global risk aversion from Middle East tensions strengthening the US dollar.

item.title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
item.description: Global nickel prices retreated 1.08% to $16,853.62 per ton amid Middle East tensions inflating costs and strengthening the US dollar. Downside is capped by Indonesia’s ore production quotas and supply risks. Analysts forecast prices to settle in a $15,000-$16,000 range due to ongoing surpluses.

item.title: Bare Bright Copper Scrap Prices – as of March 21, 2026
item.description: Bare bright copper scrap prices reported as of March 21, 2026, with national average around $3.66/lb and local yard prices ranging from $4.20/lb to $6.00/lb in recent days. Users can track daily national scrap prices and 30-day history via the iScrap App. Prices reflect market trends in scrap metal recycling.

item.title: Price LME Copper March 2026 forward (trade count)
item.description: LME Copper March 2026 forward showed bid at $13,009, ask at $13,027.50, and trade at $13,041 with cumulative volume of 165 and trade size of 1. This reflects latest forward market trading activity for copper. Data provides real-time insights into LME copper futures.

item.title: Next Phase of Aluminium Industry by Dr. Anupam Agnihotri
item.description: Dr. Agnihotri discusses technology, transition, and transformation in the aluminum industry, emphasizing power decarbonization as key to reducing carbon footprint linked to electricity mix. Incremental improvements like smelter efficiency, renewable integration, and scaling secondary recycling (using 95% less energy) are highlighted as cost-effective paths. Inert anode technologies are expected by 2030 for further decarbonization.

item.title: Live Nickel Prices Today
item.description: Global nickel prices retreated 1.08% to $16,853.62 per ton, with Chinese markets at ¥116,056 per ton. This pullback reflects risk-off sentiment from Middle East tensions and a stronger US dollar, alongside global oversupply. Downside is capped by Indonesia’s reduced ore quotas and steady Chinese stainless steel demand.

item.title: Asia's EVolution: No clear skies in Indonesia's nickel mining towns
item.description: Coverage of Indonesia's nickel mining sector and its environmental and operational challenges in mining towns.

item.title: NOVONIX’s Patented All-Dry, Zero-Waste Cathode Synthesis Technology
item.description: Dr. Mark McArthur from NOVONIX discusses their patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology for nickel-based cathodes. This innovation is expected to significantly reduce costs and environmental impact in battery production. It aligns with sustainability goals in nickel technology for electric vehicles.

item.title: Nickel-Containing Stainless Steel for Green Hydrogen Production
item.description: Green hydrogen offers unlimited potential as a fossil fuel replacement for net-zero goals. Nickel-containing stainless steel plays a key sustainable role in this transition. The article highlights its importance in climate-neutral future technologies.

item.title: SMM Nickel Midday Review - March 23, 2026
item.description: On March 23, nickel prices rose slightly as geopolitical tensions influenced market sentiment, with Trump demanding that Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours. This news item indicates ongoing price volatility driven by both supply-side factors and macroeconomic developments.

item.title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
item.description: As of March 23, 2026, LME aluminum spot prices hit $3,220 per tonne amid supply deficits, China's production caps, and surging demand from EVs and renewables. The EU's CBAM has created a green premium of $150 per tonne, benefiting low-carbon producers like Alcoa and Rio Tinto. Downstream industries face sustained high costs.

item.title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3,000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
item.description: Aluminum prices breached $3,000 per tonne as of March 23, 2026, driven by China's shift to net importer status due to its capacity-for-capacity replacement policy and surging demand from EV and solar industries. The market is experiencing unprecedented volatility with a structural deficit, prompting companies to explore near-shoring, vertical integration, and circular economy solutions including recycling infrastructure expansion.

item.title: The Aluminum Renaissance: A Deep-Dive into Alcoa's (AA) 2026 Strategic Pivot
item.description: Alcoa is advancing its competitive position through its Sustana line of low-carbon products, including EcoSource (the world's first low-carbon alumina) and EcoLum (aluminum produced with less than 4.0 kg CO2e per kg). The company's ELYSIS joint venture with Rio Tinto has moved into extended validation phase with carbon-free smelting technology that emits pure oxygen instead of CO2, positioning Alcoa as a future technology licensor for the global smelting industry.

item.title: Nickel-zinc batteries considered the new data centre gold standard
item.description: Nickel-zinc batteries are emerging as a gold standard for data centers strained by AI demands, offering high power density and sustainability advantages. The article highlights their role in balancing performance with environmental goals as data centers expand. This innovation supports efficient, eco-friendly power solutions for high-intensity computing.

item.title: Nickel-zinc and the next era of sustainable data centre power in Europe
item.description: As AI and cloud computing grow in Europe, nickel-zinc batteries are positioned to deliver sustainable, high-density power for data centers facing sustainability pressures. The technology addresses the need for efficient energy storage amid rising power demands. It represents a shift toward greener infrastructure in the region.

item.title: Raw steel production ticks higher, nears multi-year high
item.description: US domestic raw steel production rose to 1,781,000 short tons for the week ending March 21, up 0.4% from the prior week, approaching the four-year high of 1,817,000 tons from mid-February. Year-to-date production reached 20,394,000 tons, 4.9% higher than the same period in 2025, with mill capability utilization at 77.0%. Production increased in the Midwest and South regions but declined in others.

item.title: Aluminium Industry in 2026: Global Trends, Innovations, and What Buyers Should Expect
item.description: Aluminum has become a strategic material driving global manufacturing and clean energy transition, with 100 percent recyclability and no loss of properties. Leading manufacturers are adopting advanced die manufacturing, precision billet casting, AI-enabled process monitoring, and real-time extrusion parameter optimization to improve quality and reduce production variability.

item.title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
item.description: The aluminum scrap industry is undergoing a circular economy revolution, with companies investing in closed-loop recycling, green smelting technologies, and advanced sorting systems. Recycled aluminum uses up to 95% less energy than primary production, and demand is booming in Asia-Pacific, with China sourcing about 60% of its building-sector aluminum from recycled materials.

item.title: US ALUMINUM: Midstream leaders look to data center boom as EV trend falters
item.description: US midstream aluminum manufacturers are shifting focus from electric vehicles to data center construction, driven by AI infrastructure demand. EV and solar-related aluminum demand is expected to drop in 2026 following the end of US incentives, while data center investments are seen as a strategic necessity.

item.title: Copper Scrap Scrap Prices – as of March 24, 2026
item.description: Copper scrap prices are listed at $3.96/lb as of March 24, 2026, with variations by type such as Light Copper at $3.37/lb and Enameled Copper at $2.35/lb. These prices reflect current market conditions for scrap yards. The data provides insights into secondary market trends amid primary copper price declines.

item.title: Metal prices on March 24, 2026: Precious metals experience significant correction, steel prices maintain upward trend
item.description: Steel prices maintained an upward trend on March 24, 2026, while precious metals saw significant corrections. This reflects ongoing positive momentum in the steel market amid broader metal price adjustments.

item.title: Prysmian Creates World's First Negative-Carbon-Footprint Cable
item.description: Prysmian has created the world's first cable with a negative carbon footprint, representing a significant innovation in sustainable cable manufacturing. This development aligns with the company's commitment to advancing environmentally responsible energy infrastructure solutions.

item.title: A big reason the U.S. still needs Canada? Our aluminum
item.description: US tariffs on Canadian aluminum have reached 50%, driving US prices above $5,000 per ton compared to $3,000 without tariffs. This highlights ongoing US dependence on Canadian supply amid CUSMA negotiations.

item.title: US raw steel production increases by 0.4 percent - week 12, 2026
item.description: US raw steel production for the week ending March 21, 2026, was 1.781 million net tons, up 0.4% from 1.774 million net tons the previous week, with capacity utilization at 77.0%. This marks a 4.6% increase from the same week in 2025. Adjusted year-to-date production through March 21 was 20.394 million net tons, up 4.9% from last year at 77.2% utilization.

item.title: Copper prices drop amid global growth fears, plump stocks
item.description: Copper prices fell 9.4% to $12,081.74/metric ton as of March 23 due to fears of weak economic growth triggered by the US-Israel war with Iran and high energy prices. Warehouse stocks are growing after a period of tightness, adding downward pressure on prices. BMO Capital Markets downgraded their near-term copper outlook, citing disruption to the previously constructive macro setup for 2026.

item.title: Prysmian develops world's first cable with negative carbon footprint
item.description: Prysmian announced the development of the world's first cable with a negative carbon footprint, using a cradle-to-gate approach where raw material procurement and manufacturing remove more CO2 than they generate. The new cables can integrate into existing grids and will debut within Prysmian's Power Grid business in the second half of 2026.

item.title: Aluminium Prices Soar as Tariffs and Energy Costs Reshape the Market
item.description: Aluminium prices hit near four-year highs due to tariffs, high energy costs, and strong demand, benefiting US producers like Alcoa and Century Aluminum. Goldman Sachs raised its H1 2026 forecast to $3,150/ton, while US premiums exceeded $1/pound. Downstream sectors face higher costs and supply risks.

item.title: Green grant awarded: Advancing circular aluminium for sustainable water treatment
item.description: Alumichem has received a green grant to develop a circular production process for aluminium chlorohydrate (ACH) by replacing virgin aluminium with recycled aluminium. The project, supported by Denmark's Environmental Technology Development and Demonstration Programme, aims to reduce energy use, CO₂ emissions, and material waste while maintaining high quality for drinking water applications.

item.title: Next-Generation Smelting Technology for Sustainable Production
item.description: Century Aluminum and Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA) are partnering to build a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma using next-generation EX smelting technology designed to improve productivity and reduce energy consumption and emissions. The facility is expected to produce up to 750,000 metric tons annually, with construction beginning as early as 2026 and commercial production anticipated before the end of the decade.

item.title: SMM Nickel Midday Review: Nickel Prices Rebounded Sharply on March 25 as the US Was Considering Pushing for a One-Month C
item.description: On March 25, the SMM price of #1 refined nickel rose by 1,650 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Spot premiums adjusted accordingly amid the rebound. The price surge is linked to US considerations for a one-month policy push.

item.title: Indonesian nickel firms to re-apply for 2026-27 permits
item.description: Indonesian nickel mining firms are required to re-apply for their RKAB production permits for 2026-27 between 1 October and 15 November, while 2025 approvals remain unchanged. This regulatory update aims to manage nickel output amid global market dynamics. The move coincides with broader challenges in battery metals mining due to diesel supply disruptions.

item.title: Prysmian signs €2.3 billion contract for UK power link project
item.description: Italian cable maker Prysmian has signed a €2.3 billion contract with SP Energy Networks and National Grid to build the Eastern Green Link 4, a power link connecting the electrical grids of Scotland and England. This represents a significant infrastructure project for the company specializing in energy and telecom cable systems.

item.title: This ASX mining stock just jumped. Here's what's driving the move today
item.description: Nickel Industries Ltd (ASX: NIC) shares rose 6.78% after securing approval for its 2026 nickel ore sales plan in Indonesia, known as the RKAB. The approval permits around 14.3 million wet metric tonnes of ore sales, up from 9 million previously, supporting increased output at its Hengjaya Mine and downstream projects.

item.title: Steel Imports Up 0.8% In February vs. January
item.description: The U.S. imported 1,663,000 net tons of steel in February 2026, up 0.8% from January, while finished steel imports fell 6.2%. Year-to-date imports are down significantly compared to 2025, with finished steel import market share at 15%. Over the 12-month period to February 2026, total steel imports declined 20.3%.

item.title: Global Crude Steel Output Falls 2.2% Year-on-Year in February 2026
item.description: Global crude steel production declined 2.2% year-on-year to 141.8 million tonnes in February 2026 across 69 countries reporting to the World Steel Association. Regional performance was mixed, with China experiencing a 3.6% decline despite remaining the dominant producer, while India and the United States posted notable growth of 9.7% and 4.9% respectively. Russia and other CIS nations plus Ukraine reported a sharp 10.5% decline.

item.title: Aluminum Supply Crisis: Energy Constraints & Trade Impact
item.description: A global aluminum supply crisis threatens production chains in 2026 due to energy constraints and trade impacts. The article highlights challenges affecting aluminum supply worldwide. Written by Muflih Hidayat on the current date.

item.title: Metal prices today, March 26, 2026: Copper and steel fall sharply on both exchanges
item.description: Metal prices fell sharply on the London and Shanghai exchanges today due to trade tensions. Copper and steel experienced significant declines, while silver and platinum prices rose. This reflects ongoing volatility in global metal markets amid geopolitical pressures.

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: HRC Steel fell to 1,059.08 USD/T on March 26, 2026, down 0.37% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 5.59%, and year-over-year gains stand at 19.00%. Recent updates show minor daily declines despite monthly and annual upward trends.

item.title: Indonesia to cut nickel mining quota in 2026
item.description: Indonesia's energy ministry will reduce the 2026 nickel production quota to 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million tonnes in 2025. This cut, aimed at regulating rising nickel prices, falls short of estimated 330 million tonnes consumption, potentially leading to reduced smelter output or increased imports.

item.title: EEII AG: Cancellation of the Extraordinary General Meeting of March 26, 2026
item.description: This appears to be a corporate announcement regarding EEII AG, though the search result does not provide detailed information about the cancellation or its implications.

item.title: Cogne Acciai Speciali joins United Nations Global Compact
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali announced its official membership in the United Nations Global Compact, strengthening its commitment to sustainability and corporate responsibility.[1]

item.title: War Disruptions Trigger Smelting Capacity Crisis
item.description: Middle East war has caused aluminum prices to surge past $3,500/tonne, with shuttered smelting capacity and high electricity costs due to shipping lane closures. Emirates Global Aluminium faces export blockades despite ongoing operations. US producers like Century Aluminum are restarting plants to fill the gap.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 26.03.2026
item.description: India's flat steel market remains firm, with HRC prices holding near multi-month highs amid strong demand and restocking activity. Russia's potential excise duty on imported steel and EU revisions to the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism are highlighted. U.S. steel imports rose 0.8% monthly in February, while Ukraine’s scrap export ban pressures the market.

item.title: Copper futures reverse gains amid geopolitical tension
item.description: Copper futures erased recent gains due to geopolitical tensions, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz delaying copper concentrate shipments and tightening the market. Despite strong demand from data center buildouts estimated at 475,000 metric tons in 2026, rising energy costs and slowing industrial activity pose risks. Analyst Iaccino discusses the balance between supply shocks and declining business confidence.

item.title: Steel Market Forecast 2026-2027 | Global Price Outlook & Analysis
item.description: Major regulatory developments are reshaping steel trade flows and prices in 2026–27, particularly in Europe, with ferrous scrap prices rangebound in the $350–380/t range. Little meaningful steel price recovery is expected globally in 2026 due to overcapacity, weak Chinese demand, and declining raw materials, with upturns more likely in 2027.

item.title: Barrick Mining delays copper-gold project
item.description: Barrick Mining is delaying its major copper and gold project in Pakistan due to Middle East conflict and security concerns in the region.

item.title: Metals market on March 27: Nickel and tin prices recover, asphalt prices rise strongly
item.description: On March 27, 2026, nickel prices rose 1.01% to $17,338 per ton, with a weekly increase of 2.65%. This recovery reflects positive expectations in battery and renewable energy sectors. Base metals like aluminum and zinc also saw gains.

item.title: AKP to maintain 2026 nickel output after strong 2025 growth, continues infrastructure build-out
item.description: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama Tbk (AKP), a nickel miner, plans to maintain its 2026 nickel output following strong growth in 2025 while continuing infrastructure development.

item.title: Metals market on March 27: Nickel and tin prices recover, asphalt prices surge
item.description: Global metals markets showed positive developments with nickel prices rising 1.01% to $17,338 per ton and gaining 2.65% on a weekly basis, reflecting positive expectations in battery and renewable energy sectors.

item.title: Aluminum Shortages Coming Soon || Peter Zeihan
item.description: Peter Zeihan warns that aluminum production in the Persian Gulf is shutting down due to Iranian strikes on gas fields, pipelines, and power plants, affecting smelters reliant on cheap natural gas. Facilities in the region, including some of the world's largest, are operating at less than half capacity and may fully shut down within weeks, accounting for about 9% of global primary aluminum. This shortage, combined with existing US tariffs, will drive up primary aluminum prices.

item.title: Steel rebar futures fall below CNY 3,130 per ton amid Tangshan pollution emergency
item.description: Steel rebar futures hit a two-week low as China's steelmaking hub Tangshan activated emergency pollution response measures, raising concerns about potential output curbs. Chinese steel production declined 3.6% to 76.1 million tons in February, while global output fell 2.2% to 141.8 million tons. Steel prices rose 0.13% to 3,126 CNY/T on March 27, though they remain 2.22% lower than a year ago.

item.title: MOC: Average high-speed wire rod price in China up 0.2% in Mar 16-22, 2026
item.description: Chinese high-speed wire rod prices increased 0.2% during the week of March 16-22, 2026, reflecting modest upward movement in the domestic market.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper prices fluctuated around 5.44-5.47 USD per pound on March 27, down slightly daily but poised for a weekly gain amid hopes for a diplomatic resolution to the US-Iran conflict. President Trump extended a deadline for striking Iranian infrastructure to allow negotiations, with Iran permitting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Over the past month, prices fell about 7%, though still higher year-over-year.

item.title: Iranian Steel Plants Damaged: Supply Chain Crisis Unfolds
item.description: Iranian steel plants with significant projected annual capacity have been damaged, creating supply chain disruptions. The incident impacts global steel market dynamics during a period of already-strained capacity.
